title: Human Resources Consulting Services

narrative.executive_view: HR consulting is heterogeneous: AI materially compresses generalist research, policies, analytics and engagement administration, while proprietary benchmark data, credentialed retirement work and trusted board-level advice retain value. A $1-10M EBITDA acquisition is attractive only where recurring data/plan-cycle work is firm-owned; a generic project-advisory boutique risks fee deflation and key-person leakage. The supplied 5416 occupational mix is management-consulting-heavy, so role conclusions are necessarily rebuilt hypotheses [S1][S3].
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: SHRM found 39% of surveyed organizations had AI adopted in HR functions, with another 7% intending launch that year; recruiting and HR technology were the most common practice areas. AI can assemble handbooks, analyze survey data, draft job architectures and automate engagement administration, but client negotiation, change facilitation and defensible executive recommendations need accountable humans. WorldatWork likewise describes routine-work automation and role redesign rather than broad replacement [S1][S2].
narrative.value_capture: Capture depends on revenue mix. Annual subscriptions to proprietary compensation panels and recurring plan/compliance cycles should hold price better than hours-benchmarked projects, whereas generic handbook and analysis work is increasingly contestable by clients using HR-tech and AI. I select only partial distribution and moat; credentialed human gates are not used to inflate capture, because they mainly support retained demand [S2][S3].
narrative.adoption_timing: The client function is already adopting AI but is not yet a majority effective user across HR: SHRM reports 39% adopted in HR functions. I therefore set current adoption at 39% and estimate three years to 50% effective adoption for small and midsize consulting providers, allowing for data governance, client confidentiality and integration. This is not an observed consultant-firm penetration rate [S1].
narrative.consolidation_economics: There is real acquisition activity around benefits and people advisory: Aon completed its NFP acquisition, and NFP separately acquired an HR and health-and-safety consultancy whose retiring founders handed over to a continuing director. But those platforms combine insurance brokerage, benefits and wealth with consulting, so they do not validate brokerage-like multiples for fee-only NAICS 541612. Generic service-business sold SDE data is used only as a low-quality valuation anchor [S4][S5].
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is PRESSURED, not melting. IRS describes enrolled actuaries as approved to perform ERISA-required actuarial services, and proprietary data or high-trust advisory can remain paid. Conversely, SHRM reports AI use centered in HR workflows and WorldatWork reports automation of routine work; the broad, unprotected generalist consulting shell faces self-service substitution. The score does not assume the protected segment represents most firms [S1][S2][S3].
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The code boundary is unusually severe: benefits brokerage, PEO, payroll, staffing and management consulting can appear in acquisition and revenue data but are different businesses. The supplied 4-digit 5416 role mix omits distinctive compensation analysts and actuaries. The selected multiples are generic service SDE, owner-age is a proxy-free low-confidence estimate, and the decisive diligence question is recurring proprietary-data/credentialed revenue versus one-off advisory [S4][S5].

sources.title: SHRM State of AI in HR 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Survey of 1,908 HR professionals: 39% reported AI adopted in HR functions, with use concentrated in particular HR practice areas.

sources.title: WorldatWork: AI impacting rewards, skills and compensation
sources.what_it_supports: 2026 total-rewards survey commentary: routine work is automated and roles redesigned, while current impact remains early to moderate.

sources.title: IRS Enrolled Actuary Information
sources.what_it_supports: Describes enrolled actuaries approved to perform actuarial services required under ERISA.

sources.title: Aon completes acquisition of NFP
sources.what_it_supports: Documents active middle-market acquisition across brokerage, benefits consulting, wealth and retirement advisory, and its mixed-business scope.

sources.title: NFP acquires HELPGB
sources.what_it_supports: Documents acquisition of an HR and health-and-safety consultancy and founder retirement; UK scope is stated.

sources.title: BizBuySell Service Business Valuation Multiples
sources.what_it_supports: National 2021-2025 sold service-business SDE earnings-multiple distribution; broad proxy rather than HR-consulting comp.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541612 ($10,678,955,284) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541612 ($26,539,675,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $173,623 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541612); class <5: 5,881 firms, avg revenue/firm $394,584 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $61,752 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 651 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,244,538 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $194,770 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 412 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,382,619 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $372,880 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 431 firms, avg revenue/firm $7,933,174 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,241,542 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 144 firms, avg revenue/firm $29,228,493 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,574,259 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 122 firms, avg revenue/firm $121,304,852 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $18,984,209 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 575
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Routine analysis, drafting, survey operations and client admin are AI-exposed; executive advisory and credentialed sign-off persist. Residual corrects a parent-level mix that omits HR-consulting specialties.
caveats: Supplied 4-digit 5416 mix is not a 541612 occupation census.
The protected credential/data segment may be small or large by firm.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Analysis, benchmarking preparation and documentation are heavily exposed; advisory judgment remains [S1][S2].
Internal administration automates, but principals own client and board relationships.

breakdown_by_role.role: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Research and content production are highly AI-exposed.

breakdown_by_role.role: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Proposal work automates while relationship selling persists.
Engagement administration and reporting are AI-exposed.
Routine client service and scheduling are highly automatable.
General business operations analysis and document work automate substantially.
Software work is AI-augmented but systems accountability remains.
Reconciliation and filings automate partially; review remains.
Policies, HR research and workflow administration are exposed [S1].
Residual is assumed to include junior analysts, compensation/benefits operations and admin; ERISA credentialed work constrains the fraction [S3].

pi_dist.rationale: AI lowers effort in consulting workflows but client HR-tech adoption and comparable AI tools pressure project fees.
caveats: Actual retainer, project and subscription mix is unknown.

pi_moat.rationale: Proprietary data, recurring cycles and credentials can protect a portion of savings; open generalist work is weakly protected.
caveats: Credentialed human requirements support terminal demand and are not counted again as a capture uplift.

t50_years.rationale: Observed client HR adoption is 39%, below the t50 boundary; integration and governance delay effective provider adoption.
caveats: SHRM surveys HR professionals, not NAICS 541612 firms.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: SHRM reports 39% of its surveyed organizations had AI adopted in HR functions.
caveats: Population is HR functions broadly, not small US HR consultancies.

historical_analogs.value: HRIS, survey platforms and analytics progressively automated HR administration while advisory and regulated review remained human-led.
historical_analogs.rationale: Both sources describe concentration of technology in HR workflows and role augmentation.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: No dedicated owner-age source was found; a conservative unverified judgment is retained rather than importing a mismatched professional-services statistic.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No source in this packet documents a succession shortage for HR consultancies.
caveats: Founder retirement in an acquired UK firm is not sector-wide shortage evidence.

active_consolidators.rationale: Aon/NFP activity and the documented HR consultancy acquisition establish several active platforms, without treating brokerage activity as fee-only consulting demand.
caveats: Mixed brokerage, wealth and UK activities limit NAICS specificity.

buy_mult.rationale: Cautious generic-service SDE anchor with a small premium for recurring retainers where proven.
caveats: S6 is generic national service SDE, not fee-only HR-consulting EBITDA.

exit_mult.rationale: Modest premium requires recurring firm-owned data/plan work; no brokerage-roll-up multiple is imported.
caveats: M&A sources mix brokerage and consulting; generic SDE proxy remains weak.

pricing_structure.value: Project fees and retainers, plus recurring compensation-data subscriptions and plan/compliance-cycle fees in protected subsegments.
pricing_structure.rationale: Total-rewards workflow evidence and ERISA actuarial requirements support a mixed advisory/data/credentialed model.
caveats: No industry-wide revenue-mix survey was found.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: High task exposure is paired with a three-year—not immediate—t50 because effective adoption requires governance and workflow integration.

terminal_value.justification: Credentialed ERISA work and proprietary data can survive, but routine HR advisory is exposed to AI-enabled self-service and productization [S1][S2][S3].

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: Acquisition activity is documented but explicitly not treated as proof of fee-only HR-consulting multiples; generic service multiples and owner age are disclosed as weak or unsupported.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Project fees face pass-through as analysis/drafting commoditize; recurring data and mandated-cycle work offer better price retention.

dataset_mismatch.description: Supplied 4-digit 5416 mix is management-consulting dominated and omits key compensation/actuarial specialties in 541612.
sensitivity: Protected subsegment share affects capture and terminal interpretation.

market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Mixed HR advisory and benefits consulting
market_boundary.selection_basis: The score treats fee-based consulting, excluding brokerage multiples while acknowledging blurry firm boundaries.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Changes V directly through role and residual judgments.
Changes C materially depending on recurring proprietary revenue.

reviewer_note: Diligence should segment recurring data, credentialed plan work and firm-owned client cycles from generalist project revenue before underwriting a multiple.
